Brand / Notes: NatureWise or NOW Foods. Check label -- some brands coat capsules with gluten-containing fillers. Always verify GF.
Reason: Celiac disease causes malabsorption of fat-soluble vitamins including D. Blood work January 2026 showed 38 ng/mL -- adequate but lower end. Dr. Chaudhary may recommend increasing to 3000 IU.

Brand / Notes: Slow Fe brand or generic ferrous sulfate. Confirmed GF. Do not take with calcium (blocks absorption) -- separate by 2 hours from calcium dose.
Reason: Celiac-related iron absorption impairment. Ferritin and TIBC within normal limits as of January 2026 -- current dose effective.

Brand / Notes: Any brand -- ascorbic acid is naturally GF. Confirm no wheat-derived excipients on label.
Reason: Taken to enhance iron absorption. Not otherwise needed at high dose -- this low dose is functional, not supplementary for immune purposes.

Brand / Notes: Citracal brand or generic calcium citrate. NOT calcium carbonate (carbonate requires higher stomach acid and absorbs poorly in celiac patients). Confirmed GF.
Reason: Celiac-related calcium malabsorption risk. Citrate form preferred per Dr. Chaudhary recommendation. Separate from iron dose by at least 2 hours.
